assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements under IFRS.
The identified foreign exchange exposure arises from an intragroup balance or transaction linked to an intragroup dividend receivable.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for exposure to changes in fair value of a recognised asset or liability attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk associated with a recognised asset or liability or a highly probable forecast transaction.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct top-level hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk arising from net investments in foreign operations.
